source_pr.title: types: fix uint64 overflow bug in `ConvertJSONToFloat`
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

In the origin version `ConvertIntToUint(sc, j.GetInt64(), IntergerUnsignedUpperBound(mysql.TypeLonglong), mysql.TypeLonglong)` , when `j.GetInt64()` return neg number such as `1<<63`, then `ConvertIntToUint` may treat it as overflow, this is a bug.

### What is changed and how it works?

Replace `j.GetInt64` by `j.GetUint64`

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: types: fix uint64 overflow bug in `ConvertJSONToFloat`.
fix_pattern: types: fix uint64 overflow bug in `ConvertJSONToFloat`

evidence: files: expression/integration_test.go, go.mod, my_Makefile, nohup.out, types/convert.go, types/convert_test.go, types/datum_test.go
